name: Raspberry-Filled Sandwich Cookies (Luxembourgli)
recipeIngredient: 1 Egg whites (4 to 5), completely free of yolk
1/3 cup Seedless raspberry preserves or jam
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F. Line two large baking sheets (or three smaller ones) with parchment paper.
2. Toast the almonds in the oven, stirring occasionally, for 6 to 8 minutes, or until lightly browned. Set aside to cool.
3. Lower the oven temperature to 300°F.
4. Process the cooled almonds and half cup of powdered sugar in a food processor until very fine but not oily.
5. Thoroughly mix the remaining powdered sugar into the almond mixture.
6. In a small heavy saucepan, combine granulated sugar and 3 tablespoons hot water over medium-high heat. Bring to a boil, stirring.
7. Cover the pan and continue boiling for 1 1/2 minutes to allow steam to wash away sugar crystals from the sides of the pan. Uncover and continue boiling, lifting the pan occasionally and swirling contents until the syrup thickens, bubbles vigorously, and reaches the firm ball stage (240°F on a candy thermometer).
8. Test for doneness without a thermometer by dropping a small amount of syrup into a cup of ice water; it should form a firm ball when squeezed between fingers. Set aside.
9. Beat egg whites with an electric mixer until frothy. Add cream of tartar and beat to soft peaks. Turn off the mixer.
10. Return syrup to the burner and reheat just to simmering. With mixer on high speed, pour a thin, steady stream of syrup down the side of the bowl (avoid beaters or whip as syrup may stick), adding all syrup. Add vanilla and almond extracts. Continue beating until mixture cools to lukewarm.
11. Using a rubber spatula, fold the almond mixture into the beaten egg whites. Drop small spoonfuls onto parchment-lined baking sheets, spacing about 3/4 inch apart.
12. (Alternately, pipe small rounds using pastry bag with plain or open star tip.) Place pans staggered on racks in the center of the oven. Bake for 19 to 22 minutes, reversing pans and switching racks halfway through until lightly browned and firm on top but still slightly soft inside.
13. Let cool completely on wire racks. Remove from paper. Store airtight until assembly.
14. Prepare buttercream by beating together butter, powdered sugar, and preserves until light and fluffy. Spread with knife or pipe small amounts onto flat sides of half cookies. Top with remaining meringues, pressing tightly.
15. Store airtight in refrigerator for up to 24 hours; cookies will become too soft if left longer. (For longer storage, freeze wrapped tightly for up to 2 weeks; thaw before serving.) Allow to warm slightly before serving.
Makes about forty 1 1/4-inch sandwich cookies.
